page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr9i.16xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6982P-C processors. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

The instance does not include local storage, requiring external network-attached storage solutions for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 36 Gbps. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this configuration. The lack of local storage and hardware acceleration represents a tradeoff, positioning this instance primarily for memory-intensive and network-dependent workloads rather than local high-IOPS storage or parallel GPU processing.

Given its high memory-to-CPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.hfr9i.16xlarge is suited for memory-optimized workloads. These include large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise resource planning applications, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high memory density offer predictable performance for enterprise workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr9i.16xlarge is an x86_64 server instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6982P-C processors running at 3.4 GHz. It is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and operates on the KVM hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 36 Gbps. With its dedicated CPU allocation and high memory capacity, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as large databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring predictable compute performance without hyperthreading.
